"""
Regression test for Qt 6.4 dark mode text brightness threshold setting.

Issue: When users configure colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text in Qt 6.4,
the setting has no effect on dark mode rendering.

Root cause: In Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed the TextBrightnessThreshold setting to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will
ignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.

This test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).

See: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100
"""

import pytest
from qutebrowser.config import configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    """Patch the backend to QtWebEngine for testing."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


class TestQt64ThresholdTextMapping:
    """Test that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped for Qt 6.4."""

    def test_qt64_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold(self, configdata_init, config_stub):
        """
        Verify that Qt 6.4 uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.

        This test will FAIL if the threshold.text setting is not correctly mapped,
        which would cause the setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4.
        """
        # Enable dark mode
        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True

        # Set a custom threshold.text value (non-default)
        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.4
        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')
        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

        # Extract the dark-mode-settings (where the threshold is stored for Qt 6.3+)
        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])
        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

        # Qt 6.4 should use ForegroundBrightnessThreshold (not TextBrightnessThreshold)
        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (
            f"FAIL: 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not found in Qt 6.4 settings. "
            f"Full settings: {dark_mode_settings_list}. "
            f"This means the threshold.text setting will be ignored by Chromium in Qt 6.4."
        )

        # Verify the value is correct
        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
            f"Expected '100', got '{dark_mode_settings.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}'"
        )

        # Ensure the old TextBrightnessThreshold is NOT present
        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (
            f"BUG: Qt 6.4 should not use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it was found. "
            f"This is the Qt 6.3 key name. Chromium won't recognize it in Qt 6.4."
        )

    def test_qt63_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold(self, configdata_init, config_stub):
        """
        Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).

        This test ensures that the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.
        """
        # Enable dark mode
        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True

        # Set the same threshold value for comparison
        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.3
        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3')
        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

        # Extract the dark-mode-settings
        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])
        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

        # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold
        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (
            f"Expected 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in Qt 6.3 settings, but got: {dark_mode_settings_list}"
        )

        # Verify the value is correct
        assert dark_mode_settings['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'

        # Qt 6.3 should NOT have the new name
        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings

    def test_qt64_threshold_values_range(self, configdata_init, config_stub):
        """
        Verify that the threshold.text setting works with various values for Qt 6.4.

        This ensures the mapping works correctly across the valid range (0-256).
        """
        # Enable dark mode
        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True

        # Test with different threshold values
        test_values = [0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 256]

        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')

        for test_value in test_values:
            config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', test_value)

            settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])
            dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))

            # Verify the correct key and value
            assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (
                f"Missing ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for value {test_value}"
            )
            assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == str(test_value), (
                f"Expected '{test_value}', got '{dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold']}'"
            )

            # Ensure old key is not present
            assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (
                f"TextBrightnessThreshold should not be present (bug) for value {test_value}"
            )

    def test_threshold_background_also_renamed_in_qt64(self, configdata_init, config_stub):
        """
        Verify that the background threshold setting is handled correctly for Qt 6.4.

        Note: Only the text threshold was renamed. Background threshold remains
        BackgroundBrightnessThreshold, but we should verify this for consistency.
        """
        # Enable dark mode
        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True

        # Set both thresholds
        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 150)
        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.background', 100)

        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')
        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])
        dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))

        # Text threshold should use the new name in Qt 6.4
        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings
        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '150'

        # Background threshold should remain unchanged
        assert 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings
        assert dark_mode_settings['BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'

        # Verify old text key is not present
        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings

    """Create a mock config object."""
    mock_config = MagicMock()
    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    mock_config.get.return_value = usertypes.UNSET
    return mock_config

        if key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled':

            return 100
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme':
            return usertypes.UNSET
        else:
            return fallback if fallback is not False else usertypes.UNSET

"""
Regression test for Qt 6.4 dark mode text brightness threshold setting.

Issue: When users configure colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text in Qt 6.4,
the setting has no effect on dark mode rendering.

Root cause: In Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed the TextBrightnessThreshold setting to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will
ignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.

This test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).

See: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100
"""

from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode


def test_qt64_definition_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold():
    """
    Verify that the Qt 6.4 definition uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.

    This test inspects the darkmode module's internal definitions to ensure the
    threshold.text setting is correctly mapped for Qt 6.4.
    """
    # Get the Qt 6.4 definition
    qt64_def = darkmode._DEFINITIONS[darkmode.Variant.qt_64]

    # Check that the definition has the correct setting
    found = False
    for switch_name, setting in qt64_def.prefixed_settings():
        if setting.option == 'threshold.text':
            found = True
            # The chromium_key should be ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4
            assert setting.chromium_key == 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', (
                f"BUG: Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', "
                f"but found '{setting.chromium_key}'. This is the bug that causes "
                f"the threshold.text setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4."
            )
            break

    assert found, "threshold.text setting not found in Qt 6.4 definition"


def test_qt63_definition_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold():
    """
    Verify that the Qt 6.3 definition still uses TextBrightnessThreshold.

    This ensures the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.
    """
    # Get the Qt 6.3 definition
    qt63_def = darkmode._DEFINITIONS[darkmode.Variant.qt_63]

    # Check that the definition has the correct setting
    found = False
    for switch_name, setting in qt63_def.prefixed_settings():
        if setting.option == 'threshold.text':
            found = True
            # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold
            assert setting.chromium_key == 'TextBrightnessThreshold', (
                f"Qt 6.3 should use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', "
                f"but found '{setting.chromium_key}'"
            )
            break

    assert found, "threshold.text setting not found in Qt 6.3 definition"


def test_threshold_background_not_affected():
    """
    Verify that the background threshold setting is not affected by Qt 6.4 change.

    Only the text threshold was renamed. Background threshold remains BackgroundBrightnessThreshold.
    """
    # Get the Qt 6.4 definition
    qt64_def = darkmode._DEFINITIONS[darkmode.Variant.qt_64]

    # Find both threshold settings
    text_setting = None
    background_setting = None

    for switch_name, setting in qt64_def.prefixed_settings():
        if setting.option == 'threshold.text':
            text_setting = setting
        elif setting.option == 'threshold.background':
            background_setting = setting

    # Verify text threshold uses new name
    assert text_setting is not None, "threshold.text setting not found"
    assert text_setting.chromium_key == 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', (
        f"Qt 6.4 text threshold should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', "
        f"but found '{text_setting.chromium_key}'"
    )

    # Verify background threshold still uses old name
    assert background_setting is not None, "threshold.background setting not found"
    assert background_setting.chromium_key == 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold', (
        f"Qt 6.4 background threshold should be 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold', "
        f"but found '{background_setting.chromium_key}'"
    )


def test_qt515_3_qt62_unaffected():
    """
    Verify that Qt 5.15.3 and 6.2 definitions are not affected by the Qt 6.4 change.
    """
    # Get definitions for earlier versions
    qt515_3_def = darkmode._DEFINITIONS[darkmode.Variant.qt_515_3]

    # Check Qt 5.15.3
    for switch_name, setting in qt515_3_def.prefixed_settings():
        if setting.option == 'threshold.text':
            assert setting.chromium_key == 'TextBrightnessThreshold', (
                f"Qt 5.15.3 should use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', "
                f"but found '{setting.chromium_key}'"
            )
            break


def test_variant_detection():
    """
    Verify that the variant detection correctly identifies Qt 6.4.
    """
    from qutebrowser.utils import version, utils

    # Create a Qt 6.4 version object
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions(
        webengine=utils.VersionNumber(6, 4),
        chromium='120.0.0.0',
        source='test'
    )

    # Verify it detects Qt 6.4
    detected_variant = darkmode._variant(versions)
    assert detected_variant == darkmode.Variant.qt_64, (
        f"Expected Variant.qt_64, but got {detected_variant}"
    )

    # Verify Qt 6.3 is detected as qt_63
    versions_63 = version.WebEngineVersions(
        webengine=utils.VersionNumber(6, 3),
        chromium='120.0.0.0',
        source='test'
    )
    detected_variant_63 = darkmode._variant(versions_63)
    assert detected_variant_63 == darkmode.Variant.qt_63, (
        f"Expected Variant.qt_63, but got {detected_variant_63}"
    )

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-or-later

"""Get darkmode arguments to pass to Qt.

Overview of blink setting names based on the Qt version:

Qt 5.10 (UNSUPPORTED)
---------------------

First implementation, called "high contrast mode".

- highContrastMode (kOff/kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/kInvertLightness)
- highContrastGrayscale (bool)
- highContrastContrast (float)
- highContractImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone)

Qt 5.11, 5.12, 5.13 (UNSUPPORTED)
---------------------------------

New "smart" image policy.

- Mode/Grayscale/Contrast as above
- highContractImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone/kFilterSmart [new!])

Qt 5.14 (UNSUPPORTED)
---------------------

Renamed to "darkMode".

- darkMode (kOff/kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/kInvertLightness/
            kInvertLightnessLAB [new!])
- darkModeGrayscale (bool)
- darkModeContrast (float)
- darkModeImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone/kFilterSmart)
- darkModePagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterByBackground) [new!]
- darkModeTextBrightnessThreshold (int) [new!]
- darkModeBackgroundBrightnessThreshold (int) [new!]
- darkModeImageGrayscale (float) [new!]

Qt 5.15.0 and 5.15.1 (UNSUPPORTED)
----------------------------------

"darkMode" split into "darkModeEnabled" and "darkModeInversionAlgorithm".

- darkModeEnabled (bool) [new!]
- darkModeInversionAlgorithm (kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/
                                 kInvertLightness/kInvertLightnessLAB)
- Rest (except darkMode) as above.
- NOTE: smart image policy is broken with Qt 5.15.0!

Qt 5.15.2
---------

Prefix changed to "forceDarkMode".

- As with Qt 5.15.0 / .1, but with "forceDarkMode" as prefix.

Qt 5.15.3
---------

Settings split to new --dark-mode-settings switch:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2390588

- Everything except forceDarkModeEnabled goes to the other switch.
- Algorithm uses a different enum with kOff gone.
- No "forceDarkMode" prefix anymore.

Removed DarkModePagePolicy:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2323441

"prefers color scheme dark" changed enum values:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2232922

- Now needs to be 0 for dark and 1 for light
  (before: 0 no preference / 1 dark / 2 light)

Qt 6.2
------

No significant changes over 5.15.3

Qt 6.3
------

- New IncreaseTextContrast:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2893236

Qt 6.4
------

- Renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold

  "Correct brightness threshold of darkmode color classifier"
  https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100

  "Rename text_classifier to foreground_classifier"
  https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3226389
"""

import os
import copy
import enum
import dataclasses
import collections
from typing import (Any, Iterator, Mapping, MutableMapping, Optional, Set, Tuple, Union,
                    Sequence, List)

from qutebrowser.config import config
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, utils, log, version


_BLINK_SETTINGS = 'blink-settings'


class Variant(enum.Enum):

    """A dark mode variant."""

    qt_515_2 = enum.auto()
    qt_515_3 = enum.auto()
    qt_63 = enum.auto()
    qt_64 = enum.auto()


# Mapping from a colors.webpage.darkmode.algorithm setting value to
# Chromium's DarkModeInversionAlgorithm enum values.
_ALGORITHMS = {
    # 0: kOff (not exposed)
    # 1: kSimpleInvertForTesting (not exposed)
    'brightness-rgb': 2,  # kInvertBrightness
    'lightness-hsl': 3,  # kInvertLightness
    'lightness-cielab': 4,  # kInvertLightnessLAB
}
# Qt >= 5.15.3, based on dark_mode_settings.h
_ALGORITHMS_NEW = {
    # 0: kSimpleInvertForTesting (not exposed)
    'brightness-rgb': 1,  # kInvertBrightness
    'lightness-hsl': 2,  # kInvertLightness
    'lightness-cielab': 3,  # kInvertLightnessLAB
}

# Mapping from a colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images setting value to
# Chromium's DarkModeImagePolicy enum values.
# Values line up with dark_mode_settings.h for 5.15.3+.
_IMAGE_POLICIES = {
    'always': 0,  # kFilterAll
    'never': 1,  # kFilterNone
    'smart': 2,  # kFilterSmart
}

# Mapping from a colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.page setting value to
# Chromium's DarkModePagePolicy enum values.
_PAGE_POLICIES = {
    'always': 0,  # kFilterAll
    'smart': 1,  # kFilterByBackground
}

_BOOLS = {
    True: 'true',
    False: 'false',
}

_INT_BOOLS = {
    True: '1',
    False: '0',
}


@dataclasses.dataclass
class _Setting:

    """A single dark mode setting."""

    option: str
    chromium_key: str
    mapping: Optional[Mapping[Any, Union[str, int]]] = None

    def _value_str(self, value: Any) -> str:
        if self.mapping is None:
            return str(value)
        return str(self.mapping[value])

    def chromium_tuple(self, value: Any) -> Tuple[str, str]:
        return self.chromium_key, self._value_str(value)

    def with_prefix(self, prefix: str) -> '_Setting':
        return _Setting(
            option=self.option,
            chromium_key=prefix + self.chromium_key,
            mapping=self.mapping,
        )


class _Definition:

    """A collection of dark mode setting names for the given QtWebEngine version.

    Attributes:
        _settings: A list of _Setting objects for this definition.
        mandatory: A set of settings which should always be passed to Chromium, even if
                   not customized from the default.
        prefix: A string prefix to add to all Chromium setting names.
        switch_names: A dict mapping option names to the Chromium switch they belong to.
                      None is used as fallback key, i.e. for settings not in the dict.
    """

    def __init__(
            self,
            *args: _Setting,
            mandatory: Set[str],
            prefix: str,
            switch_names: Mapping[Optional[str], str] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self._settings = args
        self.mandatory = mandatory
        self.prefix = prefix

        if switch_names is not None:
            self._switch_names = switch_names
        else:
            self._switch_names = {None: _BLINK_SETTINGS}

    def prefixed_settings(self) -> Iterator[Tuple[str, _Setting]]:
        """Get all "prepared" settings.

        Yields tuples which contain the Chromium setting key (e.g. 'blink-settings' or
        'dark-mode-settings') and the corresponding _Settings object.
        """
        for setting in self._settings:
            switch = self._switch_names.get(setting.option, self._switch_names[None])
            yield switch, setting.with_prefix(self.prefix)

    def copy_with(self, attr: str, value: Any) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with a changed attribute.

        NOTE: This does *not* copy the settings list. Both objects will reference the
        same (immutable) tuple.
        """
        new = copy.copy(self)
        setattr(new, attr, value)
        return new

    def copy_add_setting(self, setting: _Setting) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with an additional setting."""
        new = copy.copy(self)
        new._settings = self._settings + (setting,)  # pylint: disable=protected-access
        return new

    def copy_replace_setting(self, option: str, chromium_key: str) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with `old` replaced by `new`.

        If `old` is not in the settings list, return the old _Definition object.
        """
        new = copy.deepcopy(self)

        for setting in new._settings:  # pylint: disable=protected-access
            if setting.option == option:
                setting.chromium_key = chromium_key
                return new

        raise ValueError(f"Setting {option} not found in {self}")


# Our defaults for policy.images are different from Chromium's, so we mark it as
# mandatory setting.

_DEFINITIONS: MutableMapping[Variant, _Definition] = {
    Variant.qt_515_2: _Definition(
        _Setting('enabled', 'Enabled', _BOOLS),
        _Setting('algorithm', 'InversionAlgorithm', _ALGORITHMS),

        _Setting('policy.images', 'ImagePolicy', _IMAGE_POLICIES),
        _Setting('contrast', 'Contrast'),
        _Setting('grayscale.all', 'Grayscale', _BOOLS),

        _Setting('policy.page', 'PagePolicy', _PAGE_POLICIES),
        _Setting('threshold.text', 'TextBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('threshold.background', 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('grayscale.images', 'ImageGrayscale'),

        mandatory={'enabled', 'policy.images'},
        prefix='forceDarkMode',
    ),

    Variant.qt_515_3: _Definition(
        # Different switch for settings
        _Setting('enabled', 'forceDarkModeEnabled', _BOOLS),
        _Setting('algorithm', 'InversionAlgorithm', _ALGORITHMS_NEW),

        _Setting('policy.images', 'ImagePolicy', _IMAGE_POLICIES),
        _Setting('contrast', 'ContrastPercent'),
        _Setting('grayscale.all', 'IsGrayScale', _BOOLS),

        _Setting('threshold.text', 'TextBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('threshold.background', 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('grayscale.images', 'ImageGrayScalePercent'),

        mandatory={'enabled', 'policy.images'},
        prefix='',
        switch_names={'enabled': _BLINK_SETTINGS, None: 'dark-mode-settings'},
    ),
}
_DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_63] = _DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_515_3].copy_add_setting(
    _Setting('increase_text_contrast', 'IncreaseTextContrast', _INT_BOOLS),
)
_DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_64] = _DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_63].copy_replace_setting(
    'threshold.text', 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold',
)


_SettingValType = Union[str, usertypes.Unset]
_PREFERRED_COLOR_SCHEME_DEFINITIONS: Mapping[Variant, Mapping[_SettingValType, str]] = {
    Variant.qt_515_2: {
        # 0: no-preference (not exposed)
        "dark": "1",
        "light": "2",
        # WORKAROUND for https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/QTBUG-89753
        # Fall back to "light" instead of "no preference" (which was removed from the
        # standard)
        "auto": "2",
        usertypes.UNSET: "2",
    },

    Variant.qt_515_3: {
        "dark": "0",
        "light": "1",
    },

    Variant.qt_63: {
        "dark": "0",
        "light": "1",
    },

    Variant.qt_64: {
        "dark": "0",
        "light": "1",
    }
}


def _variant(versions: version.WebEngineVersions) -> Variant:
    """Get the dark mode variant based on the underlying Qt version."""
    env_var = os.environ.get('QUTE_DARKMODE_VARIANT')
    if env_var is not None:
        try:
            return Variant[env_var]
        except KeyError:
            log.init.warning(f"Ignoring invalid QUTE_DARKMODE_VARIANT={env_var}")

    if versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(6, 4):
        return Variant.qt_64
    elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(6, 3):
        return Variant.qt_63
    elif (versions.webengine == utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 2) and
            versions.chromium_major == 87):
        # WORKAROUND for Gentoo packaging something newer as 5.15.2...
        return Variant.qt_515_3
    elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 3):
        return Variant.qt_515_3
    elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 2):
        return Variant.qt_515_2
    raise utils.Unreachable(versions.webengine)


def settings(
        *,
        versions: version.WebEngineVersions,
        special_flags: Sequence[str],
) -> Mapping[str, Sequence[Tuple[str, str]]]:
    """Get necessary blink settings to configure dark mode for QtWebEngine.

    Args:
       Existing '--blink-settings=...' flags, if any.

    Returns:
        A dict which maps Chromium switch names (blink-settings or dark-mode-settings)
        to a sequence of tuples, each tuple being a key/value pair to pass to that
        setting.
    """
    variant = _variant(versions)
    log.init.debug(f"Darkmode variant: {variant.name}")

    result: Mapping[str, List[Tuple[str, str]]] = collections.defaultdict(list)

    blink_settings_flag = f'--{_BLINK_SETTINGS}='
    for flag in special_flags:
        if flag.startswith(blink_settings_flag):
            for pair in flag[len(blink_settings_flag):].split(','):
                key, val = pair.split('=', maxsplit=1)
                result[_BLINK_SETTINGS].append((key, val))

    preferred_color_scheme_key = config.instance.get(
        "colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme", fallback=False)
    preferred_color_scheme_defs = _PREFERRED_COLOR_SCHEME_DEFINITIONS[variant]
    if preferred_color_scheme_key in preferred_color_scheme_defs:
        value = preferred_color_scheme_defs[preferred_color_scheme_key]
        result[_BLINK_SETTINGS].append(("preferredColorScheme", value))

    if not config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled:
        return result

    definition = _DEFINITIONS[variant]

    for switch_name, setting in definition.prefixed_settings():
        # To avoid blowing up the commandline length, we only pass modified
        # settings to Chromium, as our defaults line up with Chromium's.
        # However, we always pass enabled/algorithm to make sure dark mode gets
        # actually turned on.
        value = config.instance.get(
            'colors.webpage.darkmode.' + setting.option,
            fallback=setting.option in definition.mandatory)
        if isinstance(value, usertypes.Unset):
            continue

        result[switch_name].append(setting.chromium_tuple(value))

    return result

"""
Regression test for Qt 6.4 dark mode text brightness threshold setting.

Issue: When users configure colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text in Qt 6.4,
the setting has no effect on dark mode rendering.

Root cause: In Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed the TextBrightnessThreshold setting to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will
ignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.

This test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).

See: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100

The test is completely self-contained and examines the source code directly.
"""


def test_qt64_darkmode_source_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold():
    """
    Verify that the Qt 6.4 dark mode setting uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.

    This test reads the darkmode.py source code and verifies that the Qt 6.4
    definition correctly replaces TextBrightnessThreshold with
    ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for the threshold.text setting.

    If this test fails, it means the Qt 6.4 threshold.text setting will be
    ignored by Chromium, as Chromium renamed the Chromium setting in Qt 6.4.
    """
    import os

    # Read the darkmode.py source file from the repository
    source_file = '/app/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'

    # Try to read from the Sourcegraph version if local file is empty
    if os.path.getsize(source_file) == 0:
        # Fall back to reading from workspace
        source_file = '/workspace/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'

    with open(source_file, 'r') as f:
        source_code = f.read()

    # Verify that Qt 6.4 definition uses copy_replace_setting
    # to replace threshold.text with ForegroundBrightnessThreshold
    assert "_DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_64] = _DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_63].copy_replace_setting(" in source_code, (
        "Qt 6.4 definition not found or not using copy_replace_setting"
    )

    # Extract the relevant lines
    lines = source_code.split('\n')

    # Find the Qt 6.4 definition
    qt64_found = False
    uses_foreground = False

    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if "_DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_64]" in line:
            qt64_found = True
            # Check the next few lines for ForegroundBrightnessThreshold
            for j in range(i, min(i + 5, len(lines))):
                if "ForegroundBrightnessThreshold" in lines[j]:
                    uses_foreground = True
                    break
            break

    assert qt64_found, "Qt 6.4 definition not found in source code"
    assert uses_foreground, (
        "Qt 6.4 definition does not use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'. "
        "It should replace 'threshold.text' with 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' "
        "to work with Qt 6.4. Without this, the threshold.text setting will "
        "have no effect on Chromium in Qt 6.4."
    )


def test_qt63_darkmode_source_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold():
    """
    Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).

    This ensures the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.
    """
    import os

    # Read the darkmode.py source file
    source_file = '/app/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'
    if os.path.getsize(source_file) == 0:
        source_file = '/workspace/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'

    with open(source_file, 'r') as f:
        source_code = f.read()

    # Find the Qt 6.3 definition section
    lines = source_code.split('\n')

    qt63_has_text_threshold = False

    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        # Qt 6.3 definition should have TextBrightnessThreshold
        # It's created by copying qt_515_3 and adding increase_text_contrast
        if "Variant.qt_63" in line and "copy_add_setting" in line:
            # Look for the qt_515_3 definition which should have TextBrightnessThreshold
            for j in range(max(0, i - 50), i):
                if ("Variant.qt_515_3" in lines[j] and
                    "_Definition(" in lines[j]):
                    # Look for TextBrightnessThreshold in this definition
                    for k in range(j, min(j + 20, len(lines))):
                        if "TextBrightnessThreshold" in lines[k]:
                            qt63_has_text_threshold = True
                            break
                    break
            break

    assert qt63_has_text_threshold, (
        "Qt 6.3 should use TextBrightnessThreshold, but it doesn't "
        "appear in the source code"
    )


def test_qt64_rename_logic_is_correct():
    """
    Verify that the Qt 6.4 setting replacement logic is correct.

    This test checks that the copy_replace_setting method is being called
    with the correct arguments for Qt 6.4.
    """
    import os

    # Read the darkmode.py source file
    source_file = '/app/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'
    if os.path.getsize(source_file) == 0:
        source_file = '/workspace/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'

    with open(source_file, 'r') as f:
        source_code = f.read()

    lines = source_code.split('\n')

    # Find the Qt 6.4 definition
    qt64_replacement_found = False
    replacement_args_correct = False

    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if "Variant.qt_64" in line and "copy_replace_setting" in line:
            qt64_replacement_found = True
            # Check that it's replacing 'threshold.text' with 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'
            next_line = ""
            if i + 1 < len(lines):
                next_line = lines[i + 1]

            full_statement = line + " " + next_line

            if ("'threshold.text'" in full_statement and
                "'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'" in full_statement):
                replacement_args_correct = True
            break

    assert qt64_replacement_found, (
        "Qt 6.4 definition doesn't use copy_replace_setting"
    )

    assert replacement_args_correct, (
        "Qt 6.4 replacement arguments are not correct. "
        "Should be: copy_replace_setting('threshold.text', 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')"
    )


def test_threshold_background_not_renamed():
    """
    Verify that only the text threshold is renamed, not the background threshold.

    This test ensures the fix is specific to the text threshold setting.
    """
    import os

    # Read the darkmode.py source file
    source_file = '/app/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'
    if os.path.getsize(source_file) == 0:
        source_file = '/workspace/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'

    with open(source_file, 'r') as f:
        source_code = f.read()

    lines = source_code.split('\n')

    # Find qt_515_3 definition which defines background threshold
    background_threshold_key = None
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if "Variant.qt_515_3" in line and "_Definition(" in line:
            # Look for the background threshold setting
            for j in range(i, min(i + 20, len(lines))):
                if "threshold.background" in lines[j]:
                    # Extract the key name
                    if "BackgroundBrightnessThreshold" in lines[j]:
                        background_threshold_key = "BackgroundBrightnessThreshold"
                    break
            break

    # Verify that the background threshold is not changed in Qt 6.4
    assert background_threshold_key == "BackgroundBrightnessThreshold", (
        "Background threshold definition not found or incorrect"
    )

    # Verify that Qt 6.4 definition only replaces text threshold, not background
    has_only_text_replacement = True
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if "Variant.qt_64" in line:
            # Check that there's only one copy_replace_setting call
            replace_count = 0
            for j in range(i, min(i + 10, len(lines))):
                if "copy_replace_setting" in lines[j]:
                    replace_count += 1
                    if "background" in lines[j].lower():
                        has_only_text_replacement = False

            assert replace_count == 1, (
                "Qt 6.4 definition should have exactly one copy_replace_setting call"
            )
            break

    assert has_only_text_replacement, (
        "Qt 6.4 definition should only replace text threshold, not background"
    )

"""
Regression test for Qt 6.4 dark mode text brightness threshold setting.

Issue: When users configure colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text in Qt 6.4,
the setting has no effect on dark mode rendering.

Root cause: In Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed the TextBrightnessThreshold setting to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will
ignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.

This test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).

See: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100
"""

import pytest
from qutebrowser.config import configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    """Patch the backend to QtWebEngine for testing."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


class TestQt64ThresholdTextMapping:
    """Test that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped for Qt 6.4."""

    def test_qt64_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold(self, configdata_init, config_stub):
        """
        Verify that Qt 6.4 uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.

        This test will FAIL if the threshold.text setting is not correctly mapped,
        which would cause the setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4.
        """
        # Enable dark mode
        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True

        # Set a custom threshold.text value (non-default)
        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.4
        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')
        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

        # Extract the dark-mode-settings (where the threshold is stored for Qt 6.3+)
        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])
        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

        # Qt 6.4 should use ForegroundBrightnessThreshold (not TextBrightnessThreshold)
        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (
            f"FAIL: 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not found in Qt 6.4 settings. "
            f"Full settings: {dark_mode_settings_list}. "
            f"This means the threshold.text setting will be ignored by Chromium in Qt 6.4."
        )

        # Verify the value is correct
        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
            f"Expected '100', got '{dark_mode_settings.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}'"
        )

        # Ensure the old TextBrightnessThreshold is NOT present
        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (
            f"BUG: Qt 6.4 should not use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it was found. "
            f"This is the Qt 6.3 key name. Chromium won't recognize it in Qt 6.4."
        )

    def test_qt63_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold(self, configdata_init, config_stub):
        """
        Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).

        This test ensures that the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.
        """
        # Enable dark mode
        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True

        # Set the same threshold value for comparison
        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.3
        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3')
        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

        # Extract the dark-mode-settings
        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])
        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

        # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold
        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (
            f"Expected 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in Qt 6.3 settings, but got: {dark_mode_settings_list}"
        )

        # Verify the value is correct
        assert dark_mode_settings['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'

        # Qt 6.3 should NOT have the new name
        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings

    def test_qt64_threshold_values_range(self, configdata_init, config_stub):
        """
        Verify that the threshold.text setting works with various values for Qt 6.4.

        This ensures the mapping works correctly across the valid range (0-256).

    objects.backend = usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine


def test_qt64_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold():
    """
    Verify that Qt 6.4 uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.

    This test will FAIL if the threshold.text setting is not correctly mapped,
    which would cause the setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4.
    """
    setup_test_environment()

    # Enable dark mode
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True

    # Set a custom threshold.text value (non-default)
    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

    # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.4
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')
    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # Extract the dark-mode-settings (where the threshold is stored for Qt 6.3+)
    dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])
    dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # Qt 6.4 should use ForegroundBrightnessThreshold (not TextBrightnessThreshold)
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (
        f"FAIL: 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not found in Qt 6.4 settings. "
        f"Full settings: {dark_mode_settings_list}. "
        f"This means the threshold.text setting will be ignored by Chromium in Qt 6.4."
    )

    # Verify the value is correct
    assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected '100', got '{dark_mode_settings.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}'"
    )

    # Ensure the old TextBrightnessThreshold is NOT present
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (
        f"BUG: Qt 6.4 should not use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it was found. "
        f"This is the Qt 6.3 key name. Chromium won't recognize it in Qt 6.4."
    )


def test_qt63_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold():
    """
    Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).

    This test ensures that the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.
    """
    setup_test_environment()

    # Enable dark mode
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True

    # Set the same threshold value for comparison
    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

    # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.3
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3')
    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # Extract the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])
    dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (
        f"Expected 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in Qt 6.3 settings, but got: {dark_mode_settings_list}"
    )

    # Verify the value is correct
    assert dark_mode_settings['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'

    # Qt 6.3 should NOT have the new name
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings


def test_qt64_threshold_values_range():
    """
    Verify that the threshold.text setting works with various values for Qt 6.4.

    This ensures the mapping works correctly across the valid range (0-256).
    """
    setup_test_environment()

    # Enable dark mode
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True

    # Test with different threshold values
    test_values = [0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 256]

    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')

    for test_value in test_values:
        config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', test_value)

        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])
        dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))

        # Verify the correct key and value
        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (
            f"Missing ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for value {test_value}"
        )
        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == str(test_value), (
            f"Expected '{test_value}', got '{dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold']}'"
        )

        # Ensure old key is not present
        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (
            f"TextBrightnessThreshold should not be present (bug) for value {test_value}"
        )


def test_threshold_background_not_affected():
    """
    Verify that the background threshold setting is handled correctly for Qt 6.4.

    Note: Only the text threshold was renamed. Background threshold remains
    BackgroundBrightnessThreshold, but we should verify this for consistency.
    """
    setup_test_environment()

    # Enable dark mode
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True

    # Set both thresholds
    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 150)
    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.background', 100)

    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')
    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])
    dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))

    # Text threshold should use the new name in Qt 6.4
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings
    assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '150'

    # Background threshold should remain unchanged
    assert 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings
    assert dark_mode_settings['BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'

    # Verify old text key is not present
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings

"""
Regression test for Qt 6.4 dark mode text brightness threshold setting.

Issue: When users configure colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text in Qt 6.4,
the setting has no effect on dark mode rendering.

Root cause: In Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed the TextBrightnessThreshold setting to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will
ignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.

This test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).

See: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100
"""

from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode


def test_qt64_definition_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold():
    """
    Verify that the Qt 6.4 definition uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.

    This test inspects the darkmode module's internal definitions to ensure the
    threshold.text setting is correctly mapped for Qt 6.4.
    """
    # Get the Qt 6.4 definition
    qt64_def = darkmode._DEFINITIONS[darkmode.Variant.qt_64]

    # Check that the definition has the correct setting
    found = False
    for switch_name, setting in qt64_def.prefixed_settings():
        if setting.option == 'threshold.text':
            found = True
            # The chromium_key should be ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4
            assert setting.chromium_key == 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', (
                f"BUG: Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', "
                f"but found '{setting.chromium_key}'. This is the bug that causes "
                f"the threshold.text setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4."
            )
            break

    assert found, "threshold.text setting not found in Qt 6.4 definition"


def test_qt63_definition_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold():
    """
    Verify that the Qt 6.3 definition still uses TextBrightnessThreshold.

    This ensures the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.
    """
    # Get the Qt 6.3 definition
    qt63_def = darkmode._DEFINITIONS[darkmode.Variant.qt_63]

    # Check that the definition has the correct setting
    found = False
    for switch_name, setting in qt63_def.prefixed_settings():
        if setting.option == 'threshold.text':
            found = True
            # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold
            assert setting.chromium_key == 'TextBrightnessThreshold', (
                f"Qt 6.3 should use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', "
                f"but found '{setting.chromium_key}'"
            )
            break

    assert found, "threshold.text setting not found in Qt 6.3 definition"


def test_threshold_background_not_affected():
    """
    Verify that the background threshold setting is not affected by Qt 6.4 change.

    Only the text threshold was renamed. Background threshold remains BackgroundBrightnessThreshold.
    """
    # Get the Qt 6.4 definition
    qt64_def = darkmode._DEFINITIONS[darkmode.Variant.qt_64]

    # Find both threshold settings
    text_setting = None
    background_setting = None

    for switch_name, setting in qt64_def.prefixed_settings():
        if setting.option == 'threshold.text':
            text_setting = setting
        elif setting.option == 'threshold.background':
            background_setting = setting

    # Verify text threshold uses new name
    assert text_setting is not None, "threshold.text setting not found"
    assert text_setting.chromium_key == 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', (
        f"Qt 6.4 text threshold should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', "
        f"but found '{text_setting.chromium_key}'"
    )

    # Verify background threshold still uses old name
    assert background_setting is not None, "threshold.background setting not found"
    assert background_setting.chromium_key == 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold', (
        f"Qt 6.4 background threshold should be 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold', "
        f"but found '{background_setting.chromium_key}'"
    )


def test_qt515_3_qt62_unaffected():
    """
    Verify that Qt 5.15.3 and 6.2 definitions are not affected by the Qt 6.4 change.
    """
    # Get definitions for earlier versions
    qt515_3_def = darkmode._DEFINITIONS[darkmode.Variant.qt_515_3]

    # Check Qt 5.15.3
    for switch_name, setting in qt515_3_def.prefixed_settings():
        if setting.option == 'threshold.text':
            assert setting.chromium_key == 'TextBrightnessThreshold', (
                f"Qt 5.15.3 should use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', "
                f"but found '{setting.chromium_key}'"
            )
            break


def test_variant_detection():
    """
    Verify that the variant detection correctly identifies Qt 6.4.
    """
    from qutebrowser.utils import version, utils

    # Create a Qt 6.4 version object
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions(
        webengine=utils.VersionNumber(6, 4),
        chromium='120.0.0.0',
        source='test'
    )

    # Verify it detects Qt 6.4
    detected_variant = darkmode._variant(versions)
    assert detected_variant == darkmode.Variant.qt_64, (
        f"Expected Variant.qt_64, but got {detected_variant}"
    )

    # Verify Qt 6.3 is detected as qt_63
    versions_63 = version.WebEngineVersions(
        webengine=utils.VersionNumber(6, 3),
        chromium='120.0.0.0',
        source='test'
    )
    detected_variant_63 = darkmode._variant(versions_63)
    assert detected_variant_63 == darkmode.Variant.qt_63, (
        f"Expected Variant.qt_63, but got {detected_variant_63}"
    )

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-or-later

"""Get darkmode arguments to pass to Qt.

Overview of blink setting names based on the Qt version:

Qt 5.10 (UNSUPPORTED)
---------------------

First implementation, called "high contrast mode".

- highContrastMode (kOff/kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/kInvertLightness)
- highContrastGrayscale (bool)
- highContrastContrast (float)
- highContractImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone)

Qt 5.11, 5.12, 5.13 (UNSUPPORTED)
---------------------------------

New "smart" image policy.

- Mode/Grayscale/Contrast as above
- highContractImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone/kFilterSmart [new!])

Qt 5.14 (UNSUPPORTED)
---------------------

Renamed to "darkMode".

- darkMode (kOff/kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/kInvertLightness/
            kInvertLightnessLAB [new!])
- darkModeGrayscale (bool)
- darkModeContrast (float)
- darkModeImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone/kFilterSmart)
- darkModePagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterByBackground) [new!]
- darkModeTextBrightnessThreshold (int) [new!]
- darkModeBackgroundBrightnessThreshold (int) [new!]
- darkModeImageGrayscale (float) [new!]

Qt 5.15.0 and 5.15.1 (UNSUPPORTED)
----------------------------------

"darkMode" split into "darkModeEnabled" and "darkModeInversionAlgorithm".

- darkModeEnabled (bool) [new!]
- darkModeInversionAlgorithm (kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/
                                 kInvertLightness/kInvertLightnessLAB)
- Rest (except darkMode) as above.
- NOTE: smart image policy is broken with Qt 5.15.0!

Qt 5.15.2
---------

Prefix changed to "forceDarkMode".

- As with Qt 5.15.0 / .1, but with "forceDarkMode" as prefix.

Qt 5.15.3
---------

Settings split to new --dark-mode-settings switch:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2390588

- Everything except forceDarkModeEnabled goes to the other switch.
- Algorithm uses a different enum with kOff gone.
- No "forceDarkMode" prefix anymore.

Removed DarkModePagePolicy:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2323441

"prefers color scheme dark" changed enum values:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2232922

- Now needs to be 0 for dark and 1 for light
  (before: 0 no preference / 1 dark / 2 light)

Qt 6.2
------

No significant changes over 5.15.3

Qt 6.3
------

- New IncreaseTextContrast:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2893236

Qt 6.4
------

- Renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold

  "Correct brightness threshold of darkmode color classifier"
  https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100

  "Rename text_classifier to foreground_classifier"
  https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3226389
"""

import os
import copy
import enum
import dataclasses
import collections
from typing import (Any, Iterator, Mapping, MutableMapping, Optional, Set, Tuple, Union,
                    Sequence, List)

from qutebrowser.config import config
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, utils, log, version


_BLINK_SETTINGS = 'blink-settings'


class Variant(enum.Enum):

    """A dark mode variant."""

    qt_515_2 = enum.auto()
    qt_515_3 = enum.auto()
    qt_63 = enum.auto()
    qt_64 = enum.auto()


# Mapping from a colors.webpage.darkmode.algorithm setting value to
# Chromium's DarkModeInversionAlgorithm enum values.
_ALGORITHMS = {
    # 0: kOff (not exposed)
    # 1: kSimpleInvertForTesting (not exposed)
    'brightness-rgb': 2,  # kInvertBrightness
    'lightness-hsl': 3,  # kInvertLightness
    'lightness-cielab': 4,  # kInvertLightnessLAB
}
# Qt >= 5.15.3, based on dark_mode_settings.h
_ALGORITHMS_NEW = {
    # 0: kSimpleInvertForTesting (not exposed)
    'brightness-rgb': 1,  # kInvertBrightness
    'lightness-hsl': 2,  # kInvertLightness
    'lightness-cielab': 3,  # kInvertLightnessLAB
}

# Mapping from a colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images setting value to
# Chromium's DarkModeImagePolicy enum values.
# Values line up with dark_mode_settings.h for 5.15.3+.
_IMAGE_POLICIES = {
    'always': 0,  # kFilterAll
    'never': 1,  # kFilterNone
    'smart': 2,  # kFilterSmart
}

# Mapping from a colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.page setting value to
# Chromium's DarkModePagePolicy enum values.
_PAGE_POLICIES = {
    'always': 0,  # kFilterAll
    'smart': 1,  # kFilterByBackground
}

_BOOLS = {
    True: 'true',
    False: 'false',
}

_INT_BOOLS = {
    True: '1',
    False: '0',
}


@dataclasses.dataclass
class _Setting:

    """A single dark mode setting."""

    option: str
    chromium_key: str
    mapping: Optional[Mapping[Any, Union[str, int]]] = None

    def _value_str(self, value: Any) -> str:
        if self.mapping is None:
            return str(value)
        return str(self.mapping[value])

    def chromium_tuple(self, value: Any) -> Tuple[str, str]:
        return self.chromium_key, self._value_str(value)

    def with_prefix(self, prefix: str) -> '_Setting':
        return _Setting(
            option=self.option,
            chromium_key=prefix + self.chromium_key,
            mapping=self.mapping,
        )


class _Definition:

    """A collection of dark mode setting names for the given QtWebEngine version.

    Attributes:
        _settings: A list of _Setting objects for this definition.
        mandatory: A set of settings which should always be passed to Chromium, even if
                   not customized from the default.
        prefix: A string prefix to add to all Chromium setting names.
        switch_names: A dict mapping option names to the Chromium switch they belong to.
                      None is used as fallback key, i.e. for settings not in the dict.
    """

    def __init__(
            self,
            *args: _Setting,
            mandatory: Set[str],
            prefix: str,
            switch_names: Mapping[Optional[str], str] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self._settings = args
        self.mandatory = mandatory
        self.prefix = prefix

        if switch_names is not None:
            self._switch_names = switch_names
        else:
            self._switch_names = {None: _BLINK_SETTINGS}

    def prefixed_settings(self) -> Iterator[Tuple[str, _Setting]]:
        """Get all "prepared" settings.

        Yields tuples which contain the Chromium setting key (e.g. 'blink-settings' or
        'dark-mode-settings') and the corresponding _Settings object.
        """
        for setting in self._settings:
            switch = self._switch_names.get(setting.option, self._switch_names[None])
            yield switch, setting.with_prefix(self.prefix)

    def copy_with(self, attr: str, value: Any) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with a changed attribute.

        NOTE: This does *not* copy the settings list. Both objects will reference the
        same (immutable) tuple.
        """
        new = copy.copy(self)
        setattr(new, attr, value)
        return new

    def copy_add_setting(self, setting: _Setting) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with an additional setting."""
        new = copy.copy(self)
        new._settings = self._settings + (setting,)  # pylint: disable=protected-access
        return new

    def copy_replace_setting(self, option: str, chromium_key: str) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with `old` replaced by `new`.

        If `old` is not in the settings list, return the old _Definition object.
        """
        new = copy.deepcopy(self)

        for setting in new._settings:  # pylint: disable=protected-access
            if setting.option == option:
                setting.chromium_key = chromium_key
                return new

        raise ValueError(f"Setting {option} not found in {self}")


# Our defaults for policy.images are different from Chromium's, so we mark it as
# mandatory setting.

_DEFINITIONS: MutableMapping[Variant, _Definition] = {
    Variant.qt_515_2: _Definition(
        _Setting('enabled', 'Enabled', _BOOLS),
        _Setting('algorithm', 'InversionAlgorithm', _ALGORITHMS),

        _Setting('policy.images', 'ImagePolicy', _IMAGE_POLICIES),
        _Setting('contrast', 'Contrast'),
        _Setting('grayscale.all', 'Grayscale', _BOOLS),

        _Setting('policy.page', 'PagePolicy', _PAGE_POLICIES),
        _Setting('threshold.text', 'TextBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('threshold.background', 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('grayscale.images', 'ImageGrayscale'),

        mandatory={'enabled', 'policy.images'},
        prefix='forceDarkMode',
    ),

    Variant.qt_515_3: _Definition(
        # Different switch for settings
        _Setting('enabled', 'forceDarkModeEnabled', _BOOLS),
        _Setting('algorithm', 'InversionAlgorithm', _ALGORITHMS_NEW),

        _Setting('policy.images', 'ImagePolicy', _IMAGE_POLICIES),
        _Setting('contrast', 'ContrastPercent'),
        _Setting('grayscale.all', 'IsGrayScale', _BOOLS),

        _Setting('threshold.text', 'TextBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('threshold.background', 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('grayscale.images', 'ImageGrayScalePercent'),

        mandatory={'enabled', 'policy.images'},
        prefix='',
        switch_names={'enabled': _BLINK_SETTINGS, None: 'dark-mode-settings'},
    ),
}
_DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_63] = _DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_515_3].copy_add_setting(
    _Setting('increase_text_contrast', 'IncreaseTextContrast', _INT_BOOLS),
)
_DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_64] = _DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_63].copy_replace_setting(
    'threshold.text', 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold',
)


_SettingValType = Union[str, usertypes.Unset]
_PREFERRED_COLOR_SCHEME_DEFINITIONS: Mapping[Variant, Mapping[_SettingValType, str]] = {
    Variant.qt_515_2: {
        # 0: no-preference (not exposed)
        "dark": "1",
        "light": "2",
        # WORKAROUND for https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/QTBUG-89753
        # Fall back to "light" instead of "no preference" (which was removed from the
        # standard)
        "auto": "2",
        usertypes.UNSET: "2",
    },

    Variant.qt_515_3: {
        "dark": "0",
        "light": "1",
    },

    Variant.qt_63: {
        "dark": "0",
        "light": "1",
    },

    Variant.qt_64: {
        "dark": "0",
        "light": "1",
    }
}


def _variant(versions: version.WebEngineVersions) -> Variant:
    """Get the dark mode variant based on the underlying Qt version."""
    env_var = os.environ.get('QUTE_DARKMODE_VARIANT')
    if env_var is not None:
        try:
            return Variant[env_var]
        except KeyError:
            log.init.warning(f"Ignoring invalid QUTE_DARKMODE_VARIANT={env_var}")

    if versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(6, 4):
        return Variant.qt_64
    elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(6, 3):
        return Variant.qt_63
    elif (versions.webengine == utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 2) and
            versions.chromium_major == 87):
        # WORKAROUND for Gentoo packaging something newer as 5.15.2...
        return Variant.qt_515_3
    elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 3):
        return Variant.qt_515_3
    elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 2):
        return Variant.qt_515_2
    raise utils.Unreachable(versions.webengine)


def settings(
        *,
        versions: version.WebEngineVersions,
        special_flags: Sequence[str],
) -> Mapping[str, Sequence[Tuple[str, str]]]:
    """Get necessary blink settings to configure dark mode for QtWebEngine.

    Args:
       Existing '--blink-settings=...' flags, if any.

    Returns:
        A dict which maps Chromium switch names (blink-settings or dark-mode-settings)
        to a sequence of tuples, each tuple being a key/value pair to pass to that
        setting.
    """
    variant = _variant(versions)
    log.init.debug(f"Darkmode variant: {variant.name}")

    result: Mapping[str, List[Tuple[str, str]]] = collections.defaultdict(list)

    blink_settings_flag = f'--{_BLINK_SETTINGS}='
    for flag in special_flags:
        if flag.startswith(blink_settings_flag):
            for pair in flag[len(blink_settings_flag):].split(','):
                key, val = pair.split('=', maxsplit=1)
                result[_BLINK_SETTINGS].append((key, val))

    preferred_color_scheme_key = config.instance.get(
        "colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme", fallback=False)
    preferred_color_scheme_defs = _PREFERRED_COLOR_SCHEME_DEFINITIONS[variant]
    if preferred_color_scheme_key in preferred_color_scheme_defs:
        value = preferred_color_scheme_defs[preferred_color_scheme_key]
        result[_BLINK_SETTINGS].append(("preferredColorScheme", value))

    if not config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled:
        return result

    definition = _DEFINITIONS[variant]

    for switch_name, setting in definition.prefixed_settings():
        # To avoid blowing up the commandline length, we only pass modified
        # settings to Chromium, as our defaults line up with Chromium's.
        # However, we always pass enabled/algorithm to make sure dark mode gets
        # actually turned on.
        value = config.instance.get(
            'colors.webpage.darkmode.' + setting.option,
            fallback=setting.option in definition.mandatory)
        if isinstance(value, usertypes.Unset):
            continue

        result[switch_name].append(setting.chromium_tuple(value))

    return result

arguments.content:
"""
Regression test for Qt 6.4 dark mode text brightness threshold setting.

Issue: When users configure colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text in Qt 6.4,
the setting has no effect on dark mode rendering.

Root cause: In Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed the TextBrightnessThreshold setting to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will
ignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.

This test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to
ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).

See: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100

The test is completely self-contained and examines the source code directly.
"""


def test_qt64_darkmode_source_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold():
    """
    Verify that the Qt 6.4 dark mode setting uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.

    This test reads the darkmode.py source code and verifies that the Qt 6.4
    definition correctly replaces TextBrightnessThreshold with
    ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for the threshold.text setting.

    If this test fails, it means the Qt 6.4 threshold.text setting will be
    ignored by Chromium, as Chromium renamed the Chromium setting in Qt 6.4.
    """
    import os

    # Read the darkmode.py source file from the repository
    source_file = '/app/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'

    # Try to read from the Sourcegraph version if local file is empty
    if os.path.getsize(source_file) == 0:
        # Fall back to reading from workspace
        source_file = '/workspace/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'

    with open(source_file, 'r') as f:
        source_code = f.read()

    # Verify that Qt 6.4 definition uses copy_replace_setting
    # to replace threshold.text with ForegroundBrightnessThreshold
    assert "_DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_64] = _DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_63].copy_replace_setting(" in source_code, (
        "Qt 6.4 definition not found or not using copy_replace_setting"
    )

    # Extract the relevant lines
    lines = source_code.split('\n')

    # Find the Qt 6.4 definition
    qt64_found = False
    uses_foreground = False

    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if "_DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_64]" in line:
            qt64_found = True
            # Check the next few lines for ForegroundBrightnessThreshold
            for j in range(i, min(i + 5, len(lines))):
                if "ForegroundBrightnessThreshold" in lines[j]:
                    uses_foreground = True
                    break
            break

    assert qt64_found, "Qt 6.4 definition not found in source code"
    assert uses_foreground, (
        "Qt 6.4 definition does not use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'. "
        "It should replace 'threshold.text' with 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' "
        "to work with Qt 6.4. Without this, the threshold.text setting will "
        "have no effect on Chromium in Qt 6.4."
    )


def test_qt63_darkmode_source_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold():
    """
    Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).

    This ensures the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.
    """
    import os

    # Read the darkmode.py source file
    source_file = '/app/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'
    if os.path.getsize(source_file) == 0:
        source_file = '/workspace/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'

    with open(source_file, 'r') as f:
        source_code = f.read()

    # Find the Qt 6.3 definition section
    lines = source_code.split('\n')

    qt63_has_text_threshold = False

    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        # Qt 6.3 definition should have TextBrightnessThreshold
        # It's created by copying qt_515_3 and adding increase_text_contrast
        if "Variant.qt_63" in line and "copy_add_setting" in line:
            # Look for the qt_515_3 definition which should have TextBrightnessThreshold
            for j in range(max(0, i - 50), i):
                if ("Variant.qt_515_3" in lines[j] and
                    "_Definition(" in lines[j]):
                    # Look for TextBrightnessThreshold in this definition
                    for k in range(j, min(j + 20, len(lines))):
                        if "TextBrightnessThreshold" in lines[k]:
                            qt63_has_text_threshold = True
                            break
                    break
            break

    assert qt63_has_text_threshold, (
        "Qt 6.3 should use TextBrightnessThreshold, but it doesn't "
        "appear in the source code"
    )


def test_qt64_rename_logic_is_correct():
    """
    Verify that the Qt 6.4 setting replacement logic is correct.

    This test checks that the copy_replace_setting method is being called
    with the correct arguments for Qt 6.4.
    """
    import os

    # Read the darkmode.py source file
    source_file = '/app/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'
    if os.path.getsize(source_file) == 0:
        source_file = '/workspace/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'

    with open(source_file, 'r') as f:
        source_code = f.read()

    lines = source_code.split('\n')

    # Find the Qt 6.4 definition
    qt64_replacement_found = False
    replacement_args_correct = False

    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if "Variant.qt_64" in line and "copy_replace_setting" in line:
            qt64_replacement_found = True
            # Check that it's replacing 'threshold.text' with 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'
            next_line = ""
            if i + 1 < len(lines):
                next_line = lines[i + 1]

            full_statement = line + " " + next_line

            if ("'threshold.text'" in full_statement and
                "'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'" in full_statement):
                replacement_args_correct = True
            break

    assert qt64_replacement_found, (
        "Qt 6.4 definition doesn't use copy_replace_setting"
    )

    assert replacement_args_correct, (
        "Qt 6.4 replacement arguments are not correct. "
        "Should be: copy_replace_setting('threshold.text', 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')"
    )


def test_threshold_background_not_renamed():
    """
    Verify that only the text threshold is renamed, not the background threshold.

    This test ensures the fix is specific to the text threshold setting.
    """
    import os

    # Read the darkmode.py source file
    source_file = '/app/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'
    if os.path.getsize(source_file) == 0:
        source_file = '/workspace/qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py'

    with open(source_file, 'r') as f:
        source_code = f.read()

    lines = source_code.split('\n')

    # Find qt_515_3 definition which defines background threshold
    background_threshold_key = None
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if "Variant.qt_515_3" in line and "_Definition(" in line:
            # Look for the background threshold setting
            for j in range(i, min(i + 20, len(lines))):
                if "threshold.background" in lines[j]:
                    # Extract the key name
                    if "BackgroundBrightnessThreshold" in lines[j]:
                        background_threshold_key = "BackgroundBrightnessThreshold"
                    break
            break

    # Verify that the background threshold is not changed in Qt 6.4
    assert background_threshold_key == "BackgroundBrightnessThreshold", (
        "Background threshold definition not found or incorrect"
    )

    # Verify that Qt 6.4 definition only replaces text threshold, not background
    has_only_text_replacement = True
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if "Variant.qt_64" in line:
            # Check that there's only one copy_replace_setting call
            replace_count = 0
            for j in range(i, min(i + 10, len(lines))):
                if "copy_replace_setting" in lines[j]:
                    replace_count += 1
                    if "background" in lines[j].lower():
                        has_only_text_replacement = False

            assert replace_count == 1, (
                "Qt 6.4 definition should have exactly one copy_replace_setting call"
            )
            break

    assert has_only_text_replacement, (
        "Qt 6.4 definition should only replace text threshold, not background"
    )
